rationale: Re-ran my full round-1 self-consistency sweep against the fixed commit 2b55d1a7 (git status clean, HEAD pinned there). Built a fresh binary and ran `validate spec` against 085-094 + 110 again: results are IDENTICAL to round 1 -- 086/087/088/089/091/092/093/094/110 clean, 085/090 still fail with the same impacted-domains-resolve error (unrelated to R6, untouched by this fix). No new failures and no newly-cleared failures anywhere in the real corpus, confirming the BRIEF's prediction that the digit-boundary change only affects five-digit/prefix anomalies absent from the real spec set. Doc-sync: confirmed via `git diff 62cd6df3 2b55d1a7 -- .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md` that the doc file has a completely empty diff -- the fix touches only spec.go/spec_test.go, exactly as the round-2 BRIEF states. Checked doc-sync at the CORRECT per-bead gate scope (`validate docs --diff <merge-base-with-main>`, matching how internal/validate/docsync.go says complete.Run actually anchors the per-bead gate): clean, only the pre-existing missing-source-globs warning -- the architecture.md update from 62cd6df3 already covers the bead's source change, so the doc-only-in-parent-commit shape does not trip doc-sync at bead scope (checking the single fix-commit's diff in isolation does show a doc-sync warning, as expected, since that's not the gate's real scope). Ran go build ./... and go test -count=1 ./internal/validate ./internal/approve fresh: all green, including the 3 new digit-boundary subtests (five-digit-id-produces-no-diagnostic, five-digit-id-does-not-false-pass-via-four-digit-prefix, bare-id-anchor-existing-passes). No regression found in my lens.
findings: Corpus sweep (085-094 + 110) against 2b55d1a7 is byte-for-byte identical to round 1: same 9 clean, same 2 failures (085, 090) via the pre-existing impacted-domains-resolve path unrelated to this fix.

`.mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md` has a completely empty diff between 62cd6df3 and 2b55d1a7 -- the fix commit does not touch documentation, matching the round-2 BRIEF's delta description (spec.go + spec_test.go only, +72/-6).

The per-bead doc-sync gate (base = merge-base with main, matching complete.Run's real anchoring per internal/validate/docsync.go's own doc comments) is clean on the whole bead branch through 2b55d1a7 -- only the pre-existing missing-source-globs advisory. Checking the fix commit's diff in isolation (62cd6df3..2b55d1a7) does surface a doc-sync warning, but that's an artifact of checking a sub-range narrower than the actual gate scope, not a real doc-sync gap.
internal/validate and internal/approve both pass fresh (-count=1) at 2b55d1a7, and go build ./... is clean.
The 3 new/changed digit-boundary subtests in spec_test.go pass, confirming the five-digit truncation bug G1/F1 found in round 1 is fixed without introducing a new error class (parity preserved).
